trajectory.description: Observation: First iteration, no baseline yet. Analysis: ClimSim is well-suited for MLPs with skip connections; large dataset means need efficient training with big batch sizes. Decision: Build ResNet-MLP, train 3 epochs quickly, submit eval to measure baseline R² and iteration time.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training ran 3 epochs in 184s but val_r2 was -300M (clearly broken) while val_loss was 0.004 (reasonable). Analysis: The R² computation has a numerical bug - likely some features have near-zero variance causing division issues. The MSE of 0.004 on pre-normalized data implies R² ≈ 0.996, so the model is actually working. Decision: Fix R² computation, add NaN checks, use cosine LR schedule, train longer (10 epochs ≈ 10min), and submit eval.

Observation: Training val_r2=0.5564 but eval mean_r2=-3.6B due to ptend_q R²=-8.4B. Other groups are fine (ptend_t=0.61, radiation=0.96, precipitation=0.81). Analysis: A few ptend_q features (likely upper-atmosphere humidity tendency with near-zero test variance) cause catastrophic negative R² that dominates the mean. Even small prediction errors on near-zero-variance features produce R² of -billions. Decision: (1) Add output clamping to model (prevent extreme predictions), (2) Compute per-feature R² on validation set, save zero_mask for features with negative val R², (3) In predict.py, replace predictions for masked features with feature mean (~0), (4) Clip final predictions to [-5,5]. Retrain for 20 epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline mean_r2=0.5206 with ptend_q=0.3227 as the main bottleneck. The zero_mask fix works well. Model is only ~17M params with room to grow. Analysis: Scaling up the model (hidden_dim 1024→1536, n_blocks 8→10), adding input skip connection, weighting ptend_q loss 2x, and training longer (30 epochs) should improve all feature groups, especially ptend_q. Decision: Implement larger model with skip connection and feature-weighted loss, train for 30 epochs, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: mean_r2 dropped from 0.5206 to 0.2958 with larger model + skip. All feature groups regressed. Analysis: (1) Skip connection maps input states directly to output tendencies - fundamentally different quantities, so skip adds noise. (2) Checkpoint saving based on val_r2 is dominated by noisy near-zero-variance features, may save suboptimal checkpoint. (3) Feature weighting (q_weight=2.0) may cause imbalance. Decision: Remove skip, save best checkpoint based on val_loss not val_r2, use moderate architecture (1024/8), equal loss weighting, train 25 epochs.

Analysis: The winning formula was: 1024/8 architecture, no feature weighting, proper zero_mask. I need to go back to this and train longer. trajectory.description: Observation: The threshold=0.5 fix_mask.py masked ~55 features including many with positive R² (0.2-0.84), which is way too aggressive. The key issue is that features with very low variance (< 0.001) have unstable R² across val/test splits, causing catastrophic test R² even when val R² looks positive. Analysis: Need a balanced masking strategy - mask features with very low variance (< 0.005) which are risky, plus features with negative R². This should mask ~20 features (similar to iter 4 which got 0.5206). Also need to verify predict.py works correctly. Decision: Fix masking with var<0.005 OR R²<0, run fix_mask, then submit eval immediately.

trajectory.description: Observation: Last iteration's fix_mask with var<0.005 was too aggressive (~45 features masked), losing positive R² contributions from features 51-58 and 80-95. The current model from iter 13 (35 epochs) needs proper masking to evaluate. Analysis: Features 60-79 have var=0 (meaningless R²), must be masked. Features 80-87 have var 0.000001-0.000698 - risky but might contribute positively. Features 51-58 (ptend_t upper) have var 0.002-0.003 with R² 0.31-0.49 - should keep. Using var<0.001 threshold masks features 60-87 (~28 features) plus any with R²<0, which is a balanced approach between iter 4's 20 masks (got 0.5206) and the over-aggressive 45 masks. Decision: Update fix_mask with var<0.001 OR R²<0, run it, submit eval immediately to get baseline with current model.

trajectory.description: Observation: Best mean_r2=0.5229 with ptend_q=0.3217 as main bottleneck. The model (1024/8, 35 epochs) was still improving. Analysis: Two key improvements: (1) Feature-weighted loss with zero weight on near-zero-variance features (don't waste capacity on unpredictable features) and 1.5x weight on non-masked ptend_q features (direct capacity to bottleneck). (2) AMP for faster training, allowing 50 epochs in ~35 minutes. Previous q_weight=2.0 failed because it weighted ALL ptend_q including unpredictable ones; zeroing out masked features should fix this. Decision: Retrain with feature-weighted loss and AMP, 50 epochs, then fix_mask and submit eval.

trajectory.error_summary: [TIMEOUT] Command exceeded 2908s limit. Consider reducing epochs or using a smaller model.
trajectory.description: Observation: Feature-weighted loss made things worse (val_loss 0.003295→0.005600, masked R2 0.4899→0.4613). The best model (iter 18) used 1024/8, no weighting, 35 epochs and was still improving. Analysis: The winning formula is simple: standard ResNet-MLP with uniform loss, proper masking. Need to train the same config longer (60 epochs) for better convergence. Decision: Retrain with proven config (1024/8, dropout=0.1, no feature weighting) for 60 epochs, then fix_mask + eval.
